plan: Trim SPY to meet the 20% single-name mandate and reduce concentration.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to cut losses and remove downtrends.
Exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Add XLF to diversify into financials, a high-scoring setup, while maintaining a cash buffer.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer of approximately 15-20%.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and downtrending positions. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also more limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% single-name mandate and reduce concentration.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to improve portfolio health.
Reduce SOXL to a small, manageable position (5%) and sell SPAL to limit leveraged ETF risk.
Add AMD, a high-scoring tech setup, to balance growth potential with risk.
Maintain a cash buffer of around 10% to manage market fluctuations.
This plan balances growth with risk management, focusing on quality setups.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5%/month return, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality growth setups. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% single-name mandate, freeing capital for high-conviction trades.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove underperformers.
Increase SOXL to 10% (within mandate) to leverage its strong scanner read.
Sell SPAL due to its 'below stop' status, adhering to strict stop discipline.
Add XOM, a top-scoring scanner setup, to diversify and press into strength.
Maintain a small cash buffer (~5%) to stay fully invested in high-potential setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month return, aiming for stretched growth. It leans into leveraged ETFs and high-scoring setups, accepting higher risk. Expect significantly higher volatility and potential drawdowns, especially given the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to meet the 25% single-name mandate and free capital for income.
Increase QQQI to 30% to boost monthly distributions, despite its current downtrend.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, and SMH to reduce exposure to growth and downtrending assets.
Eliminate all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to minimize volatility and risk.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions over capital growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for liquidity and to smooth out market swings.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1%/month, focusing on steady distributions. It significantly reduces growth and high-beta exposure, favoring income-generating ETFs. While aiming for consistent payouts, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns. This is not a savings account, but aims for lower drawdown.
